# 🎓 DevOps Fundamentals - Student Management Portal

**Developed by:** Khushi  
**Live Demo:** [https://devops-project.khushiray.in/](https://devops-project.khushiray.in/)  
*(Note: Registration requires a valid, single-use invite code generated by an Administrator)*

---

## 📖 Project Overview
This is a secure, real-time Student Management Web Application developed for the DevOps Fundamentals coursework. It provides a centralized dashboard for students to track their academic progress and an admin panel for global data management. The project utilizes a serverless architecture focusing on database security and responsive UI design.

## ✨ Key Features

### 👨‍🎓 Student Dashboard
* **Attendance Tracking:** View attendance history and mark presence in real-time (only when the global window is enabled by the admin).
* **Academic Records:** Clean interface to track Mid-Term, End-Sem, Practical, and CAP marks.
* **Assignments:** Monitor pending and submitted assignments along with deadlines.
* **Feedback System:** Secure, one-time suggestion box for administration review.

### 👨‍💻 Admin Dashboard
* **Global Attendance Control:** Master switch to instantly open/close the attendance marking window for all students simultaneously.
* **Invite Code Generator:** Create single-use, secure registration codes for new student onboarding.
* **Student Management:** Full control over student records, attendance overrides, marks updating, and proxy flagging.
* **Feedback Inbox:** View all student suggestions centrally.

### 🛡️ Security Highlights (DevOps Focus)
* **Firestore Security Rules:** Strict backend RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) ensures students can only read/write their own data, preventing unauthorized access.
* **Single-Use Invite Codes:** Registration is locked behind transactional, one-time-use codes that are permanently burned upon use to prevent spam accounts.
* **Server Configuration (`.htaccess`):** Implements clean URL routing (removing `.html` extensions), blocks directory indexing, and enforces strict HTTP security headers.

## 🛠️ Technology Stack
* **Frontend:** HTML5, CSS3 (Mobile-First Responsive Design), Vanilla JavaScript
* **Backend / BaaS:** Firebase Authentication & Cloud Firestore (NoSQL)
* **Routing:** Apache (`.htaccess`)

## 🚀 Setup Instructions
1. Clone this repository.
2. Create a Firebase project and enable **Authentication** (Email/Password) and **Firestore Database**.
3. Update the placeholder keys in `firebase-config.js` with your actual Firebase credentials.
4. Copy the contents of `firestore.rules` and publish them in your Firebase Console to secure the database.
5. Host the files using any standard web server (e.g., Hostinger, Firebase Hosting, or VS Code Live Server).
